label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out the best title for this passage. The options are A, B, C, D. Let me read through the passage again to get a good grasp. The passage starts by saying that if you want to see the effects of global warming, go north. Will Steger is leading an expedition. He's a famous polar explorer and environmentalist who's been sounding the alarm on global warming. His team is doing a dogsled trip across Ellesmere Island, and they'll be sharing updates online so people can follow along. The team includes some known explorers like Sam Branson and Sigrid Ekran. Steger mentions that climate change has already changed the Arctic's geography and that within a decade, reaching the North Pole by dog sled would be impossible without flotation. The last sentence says that people can change, and their willingness will determine Earth's future. So the main themes here are global warming's effects, an expedition to show those effects, and the importance of human action. The question is about the best title. Let's look at the options: A) The Arctic Is In Danger B) Discoveries of the Arctic C) Sledding Through the Arctic D) Consequences of Global Warming Option A focuses on the Arctic being in danger. The passage does mention that the Arctic is being affected by global warming, but the title should encapsulate the main idea. The expedition is about showing the effects of global warming, not just the danger to the Arctic itself. Option B is about discoveries. The passage mentions the team will share their journey, but the main point isn't discoveries for the sake of knowledge; it's about demonstrating climate change impacts. Option C is about sledding. The dogsled trip is part of the story, but it's a method to show the effects of global warming. The title might be too narrow if it's just about sledding. Option D is consequences of global warming. The entire passage is centered around showing how global warming is affecting the Arctic through this expedition. Steger's goal is to take people to the front lines of global warming, so the consequences are the main topic here. So
